title: Measurement of Left Ventricular Hemodynamic Parameters in Intact Mice
author: E. Dale Abel
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.7tdhni6
materials: Left ventricular systolic pressure (LVSP) Units: mmHg (
Heart Rate Units: beats per minute (

steps.title: Left ventricular end diastolic pressure (LVEDP) Units
steps.instruction: Left ventricular end diastolic pressure (LVEDP) Units: mmHg (

steps.title: Positive dP/dt – First derivative of LV pressure
steps.instruction: Positive dP/dt – First derivative of LV pressure. This index is a measure of the rate of pressure development in the left ventricle. Units: mmHg/msec. (

steps.title: Negative dP/dt – First derivative of LV pressure
steps.instruction: Negative dP/dt – First derivative of LV pressure. This index is a measure of the rate of ventricular relaxation, or the rate of pressure decay in the LV Units: mmHg/msec.
